articles.headline: National Youth Day 2025: India Celebrates Swami Vivekananda's Legacy with Focus on Youth Empowerment
articles.explainer:
• India observes National Youth Day on January 12th, marking the birth anniversary of Swami Vivekananda, a towering figure who inspired generations.
• The theme for 2025 focuses on 'Youth for Viksit Bharat', emphasizing their role in achieving a developed India by 2047.
• Nationwide events, seminars, and workshops are being organized to promote Vivekananda's ideals of self-reliance, character building, and social service among the youth.
• Prime Minister addressed a virtual conclave, urging youth to embrace innovation, entrepreneurship, and contribute actively to nation-building.
• Government initiatives like Skill India Mission and Start-up India were highlighted as platforms for youth to realize their potential.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.

History of the world will include events from 18th century such as industrial revolution, world wars, redrawal of national boundaries, colonization, decolonization, political philosophies like communism, capitalism, socialism etc.— their forms and effect on the society.
prelimsPoints: National Youth Day is celebrated on January 12th.
It commemorates the birth anniversary of Swami Vivekananda (born 1863).
The day was first celebrated in 1985.
Swami Vivekananda's original name was Narendranath Datta.
He was a chief disciple of Ramakrishna Paramahamsa.
He established the Ramakrishna Mission in 1897.
He represented India at the Parliament of the World's Religions in Chicago in 1893.
mainsPoints: Discuss Swami Vivekananda's philosophy and its relevance in contemporary India, particularly for youth development and nation-building.
Analyze the role of youth in achieving the 'Viksit Bharat @2047' vision, citing government initiatives and challenges.
Examine how the ideals of self-reliance, character building, and social service promoted by Vivekananda can address modern societal issues among youth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government programs aimed at youth empowerment, skill development, and entrepreneurship in light of Vivekananda's vision.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of Swami Vivekananda with a group of diverse Indian youth, symbolizing inspiration and future leadership.
articles.trivia: Swami Vivekananda's famous speech at the Parliament of the World's Religions began with 'Sisters and Brothers of America...'.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Committee to Review Draft Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025
articles.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) is set to commence a detailed review of the draft rules for the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The rules, expected to be finalized by mid-2025, will lay down the operational framework for data fiduciaries and data principals.
• Key areas of discussion include consent mechanisms, data breach notification protocols, cross-border data transfers, and the powers of the Data Protection Board.
• Stakeholder consultations are ongoing to ensure a balanced approach that protects individual privacy while fostering innovation in the digital economy.
• The review aims to address concerns regarding implementation challenges and potential impacts on businesses and citizens.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) was enacted in 2023.

It aims to provide for the processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process personal data for lawful purposes.
The Act establishes a Data Protection Board of India.
Key principles include consent-based data processing, purpose limitation, and data minimization.
It applies to the processing of digital personal data within India and to certain processing outside India.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the potential impact of the DPDP Act and its upcoming rules on individual privacy rights and the digital economy in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing a robust data protection framework in a digitally diverse country like India.
Evaluate the role of the Data Protection Board of India in enforcing compliance and adjudicating disputes under the DPDP Act.
Compare India's data protection framework with international standards like GDPR, highlighting similarities and differences.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image showing digital data flowing through a secure network, with a 'privacy shield' icon.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'right to be forgotten' is included in data protection laws globally, allowing individuals to request deletion of their personal data under certain conditions.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful completion of a crucial test for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.
• The test involved a scaled-down model performing autonomous landing maneuvers, simulating re-entry conditions.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing a cost-effective and sustainable space transportation system for India.
• RLV technology aims to reduce launch costs by recovering and reusing the first and second stages of rockets, similar to SpaceX's Falcon 9.
• The long-term vision includes developing a Two Stage To Orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch vehicle.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO is developing a Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program.
RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) is a key project under this program.
The goal is to achieve cost-effective access to space.
RLV technology involves recovering and reusing launch vehicle stages.
The RLV-TD program includes several experimental missions: Hypersonic Flight Experiment (HEX), Landing Experiment (LEX), Return Flight Experiment (REX), and Scramjet Propulsion Experiment (SPEX).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's future space missions and its implications for global space competitiveness.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of developing reusable rockets compared to conventional single-use launch vehicles.
Examine the challenges and technological hurdles ISRO faces in developing and operationalizing a fully reusable launch system.
How will RLV technology contribute to India's ambition of becoming a major player in commercial space launches and space tourism?
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype descending for an autonomous landing.
articles.trivia: The Space Shuttle was the world's first operational reusable orbital spacecraft, though its reusability was complex and costly.

articles.headline: India and Japan Hold 15th Strategic Dialogue, Reaffirm Commitment to Free and Open Indo-Pacific
articles.explainer:
• India and Japan conducted their 15th Strategic Dialogue in Tokyo, focusing on enhancing bilateral cooperation and regional security.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing adherence to international law and peaceful resolution of disputes.
• Discussions covered defense cooperation, economic partnership, infrastructure development, and collaboration in emerging technologies.
• The dialogue also addressed regional challenges, including maritime security, counter-terrorism, and climate change.
• Ministers underscored the importance of Quad cooperation and strengthening supply chain resilience.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are key partners in the Quad grouping, along with the USA and Australia.
The Indo-Pacific region is a major focus for both countries' foreign policy.
Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.
Both countries participate in joint military exercises, such as 'Dharma Guardian' (Army) and 'Malabar' (Naval - Quad).
The 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' defines India-Japan ties.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership in ensuring stability and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the various pillars of India-Japan cooperation, including defense, economy, and technology, and their contributions to India's strategic interests.
Evaluate the role of the Quad in addressing contemporary geopolitical challenges in the Indo-Pacific and its implications for India's foreign policy.
Examine how India and Japan can further enhance their collaboration in areas like critical technologies, climate action, and resilient supply chains.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed prominently during a high-level diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: Japan is India's largest ODA (Official Development Assistance) provider.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs Stricter Compliance and Transparency in Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA)
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, emphasized the need for stricter compliance and enhanced transparency in the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process.
• The judgment directed regulatory bodies to ensure thorough public hearings and independent scrutiny of EIA reports before granting project clearances.
• It highlighted the importance of cumulative impact assessments for projects in ecologically sensitive zones.
• The court also mandated the establishment of a robust post-clearance monitoring mechanism to ensure adherence to environmental conditions.
• This ruling aims to strengthen environmental governance and prevent ecological degradation caused by industrial and infrastructure projects.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: EIA is a process of evaluating the likely environmental impacts of a proposed project or development, taking into account inter-related socio-economic, cultural and human-health impacts, both beneficial and adverse.
In India, EIA is statutorily backed by the Environment Protection Act, 1986.
The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) is the nodal agency.
Public consultation/hearing is a mandatory part of the EIA process for certain projects.
The Supreme Court often intervenes to ensure environmental protection through its rulings.
mainsPoints: Analyze the critical role of Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) in sustainable development and environmental protection in India.
Discuss the loopholes and challenges in the current EIA process that necessitate judicial intervention, as highlighted by the Supreme Court's ruling.
Evaluate the implications of stricter EIA compliance and increased transparency for industrial development and investment in India.
Suggest reforms to strengthen the EIA framework in India, incorporating best practices from around the world.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel striking a sound block placed on a document titled 'EIA Report', symbolizing judicial scrutiny over environmental clearances.
articles.trivia: The first formal EIA system was established in the United States with the National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) of 1969.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Atmanirbhar Agritech Mission' to Modernize Indian Agriculture
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare launched the 'Atmanirbhar Agritech Mission' aimed at revolutionizing Indian agriculture through technology.
• The mission focuses on promoting precision farming, use of AI and IoT in agriculture, climate-resilient crop varieties, and digital extension services.
• It includes provisions for subsidies and incentives for farmers to adopt modern agricultural machinery and practices.
• Special emphasis will be placed on developing indigenous agricultural technologies and strengthening farmer-producer organizations (FPOs).
• The initiative seeks to enhance farm productivity, reduce post-harvest losses, and ensure better income for farmers.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: The mission aligns with the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision.
Focuses on integrating technology like AI, IoT, drones in agriculture.
Aims to enhance productivity and reduce post-harvest losses.
Promotes climate-resilient agricultural practices.
Includes support for Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs).
mainsPoints: Analyze how the 'Atmanirbhar Agritech Mission' can address the challenges faced by Indian agriculture, such as low productivity, climate change impacts, and farmer distress.
Discuss the role of technology (AI, IoT, precision farming) in transforming Indian agriculture and contributing to food security and farmer income.
Evaluate the potential of indigenous agricultural technologies and FPOs in achieving the mission's objectives.
Examine the policy measures and investments required to ensure widespread adoption of modern agricultural practices under this mission.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer using a tablet to monitor crop health, with drones flying over fields in the background, symbolizing modern agriculture.
articles.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of rice, wheat, sugarcane, and cotton.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Policy Rates Amidst Global Economic Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its first meeting of 2025, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach amidst persistent global economic uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions and fluctuating commodity prices.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need to balance inflation management with supporting economic growth, which is showing signs of resilience.
• The MPC also kept the stance of 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Future policy actions will remain data-dependent, with a close watch on domestic inflation trends and global financial market stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body headed by the RBI Governor.
Its primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The current inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
The 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance implies that the RBI will focus on reducing liquidity to control inflation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on policy rates, considering both domestic economic indicators and global headwinds.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the objective of supporting economic growth in the current macroeconomic environment.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflation and influencing economic activity in India.
Examine the implications of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance for India's financial markets and broader economy in the short to medium term.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up of the Reserve Bank of India logo on a building facade, symbolizing monetary authority.
articles.trivia: The RBI was established in 1935 under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and was nationalized in 1949.

articles.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for Climate Resilience at UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to global climate action and pledged enhanced support for climate resilience initiatives at a key UN forum.
• The Indian delegation highlighted its domestic efforts in renewable energy deployment, afforestation, and disaster risk reduction.
• Emphasis was placed on the need for developed countries to fulfill their financial commitments to developing nations for climate adaptation and mitigation.
• India also advocated for technology transfer and capacity building to help vulnerable countries combat climate change impacts.
• The discussions focused on preparing for the upcoming COP30 summit and accelerating the implementation of the Paris Agreement goals.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India is a signatory to the Paris Agreement.
India's Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) include reducing emissions intensity and increasing non-fossil fuel electricity capacity.
The concept of 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities' (CBDR) is central to India's stance in climate negotiations.
The Green Climate Fund (GCF) is a mechanism under the UNFCCC to assist developing countries in adaptation and mitigation practices.
India has set ambitious targets for renewable energy capacity, particularly solar power.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's leadership role in global climate action, specifically its contributions to climate resilience and adaptation efforts.
Discuss the challenges faced by developing countries, including India, in achieving climate targets without adequate financial and technological support from developed nations.
Evaluate the effectiveness of international climate forums and agreements, such as the Paris Agreement, in fostering equitable and ambitious climate action.
Examine how India's domestic policies and initiatives, like the National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC), contribute to its international climate pledges.
articles.imageDescription: A globe surrounded by hands reaching out, symbolizing international cooperation on climate action, with renewable energy sources in the background.
articles.trivia: India's per capita emissions are significantly lower than those of many developed countries.

articles.headline: New National Policy on E-Waste Management Announced to Tackle Growing Electronic Waste
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change unveiled a comprehensive National Policy on E-Waste Management, effective 2025.
• The policy aims to streamline the collection, segregation, recycling, and disposal of electronic waste across the country.
• It introduces stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) norms, holding manufacturers accountable for the entire lifecycle of their products.
• Provisions for incentivizing formal recycling units and discouraging informal, environmentally harmful practices are also included.
• The policy emphasizes public awareness campaigns and skill development for safe e-waste handling and recycling.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: E-waste is defined as discarded electrical or electronic devices.
India's first E-Waste (Management and Handling) Rules were notified in 2011.
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) makes producers responsible for managing their products at end-of-life.
Improper e-waste disposal can lead to soil and water contamination due to heavy metals and toxic chemicals.
The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) oversees e-waste management in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing challenge of e-waste management in India and the environmental and health hazards associated with improper disposal.
Analyze the key provisions of the new National Policy on E-Waste Management and evaluate its potential to transform India's approach to electronic waste.
Examine the role of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) in promoting a circular economy and sustainable consumption patterns.
Suggest measures to strengthen the informal e-waste recycling sector and integrate it into a formal, environmentally sound management system.
articles.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic devices, with a recycling symbol superimposed, illustrating the e-waste problem.
articles.trivia: Globally, e-waste is the fastest-growing waste stream.

articles.headline: Project Tiger Completes 50 Years: Achievements and Future Conservation Strategies
articles.explainer:
• Project Tiger, India's flagship wildlife conservation program, marks its 50th anniversary, celebrating significant achievements in tiger population recovery.
• A comprehensive review of the project highlighted the increase in tiger numbers and the expansion of protected areas.
• Challenges such as habitat fragmentation, human-wildlife conflict, and poaching continue to pose threats to tiger conservation.
• New strategies involving community participation, advanced surveillance technology, and transboundary cooperation were discussed for the next phase of the project.
• The government reaffirmed its commitment to securing tiger habitats and biodiversity conservation across the country.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and environment.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in 1973.
It is administered by the National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA).
India accounts for about 70% of the world's tiger population.
The 'All India Tiger Estimation' is conducted every four years.
Tiger reserves are designated protected areas for tiger conservation.
The St. Petersburg Declaration (2010) aimed to double tiger numbers by 2022 (Tx2 goal).
mainsPoints: Evaluate the success of Project Tiger over the past 50 years in conserving tiger populations and their habitats in India.
Discuss the contemporary challenges to tiger conservation, including habitat loss, human-wildlife conflict, and illegal wildlife trade.
Analyze the role of community participation and technological advancements in strengthening future tiger conservation strategies.
Examine how tiger conservation contributes to broader biodiversity protection and ecological balance in India's forest ecosystems.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through dense forest, symbolizing successful conservation efforts.
articles.trivia: India's tiger population has risen significantly, crossing 3,000 in recent estimates, a testament to Project Tiger's success.

articles.headline: India Launches First Phase of National Quantum Computing Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology (DST) officially inaugurated the first phase of India's National Quantum Computing Mission (NQCM).
• This phase focuses on establishing Quantum Computing Labs and developing indigenous quantum hardware and software platforms.
• The mission aims to foster research and development in quantum technologies, including quantum communication, sensing, and metrology.
• Collaborative efforts between academia, industry, and government research institutions are central to achieving the mission's goals.
• India seeks to position itself as a global leader in quantum technologies, with significant applications envisioned across defense, healthcare, and finance.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: The National Quantum Computing Mission (NQCM) was approved in 2023.
It aims to develop quantum technologies and related applications.
Quantum computing uses principles of quantum mechanics (superposition, entanglement) to perform calculations.
Qubits are the basic units of quantum information.
Potential applications include drug discovery, materials science, cryptography, and artificial intelligence.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the National Quantum Computing Mission for India's scientific and technological advancement and its strategic implications.
Explain the fundamental principles of quantum computing and its potential applications in various sectors, including defense, healthcare, and finance.
Analyze the challenges India faces in developing indigenous quantum technologies and becoming a global leader in this frontier area.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and international collaborations in accelerating quantum research and development in India.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized representation of a quantum chip with glowing connections, symbolizing advanced technology.
articles.trivia: A qubit can exist in a superposition of 0 and 1 simultaneously, unlike a classical bit which is either 0 or 1.

articles.headline: Review of National Skill Development Mission: Progress and Future Targets for Youth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship conducted a comprehensive review of the National Skill Development Mission (NSDM) progress.
• The review highlighted achievements in skilling millions of youth across various sectors and the expansion of ITIs and Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Kendras.
• Challenges such as skill-demand mismatch, quality of training, and industry linkages were identified as areas requiring further focus.
• New targets were set to align skilling programs with emerging industry demands, particularly in green jobs and digital technologies.
• Emphasis was placed on strengthening apprenticeship programs and promoting entrepreneurship among skilled youth.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
articles.source: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
prelimsPoints: The National Skill Development Mission (NSDM) was launched in 2015.
It aims to create convergence across various skill development schemes.
Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) is a flagship scheme under NSDM.
The National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC) is a not-for-profit public limited company under the Ministry.
ITIs provide vocational training for various trades.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and achievements of the National Skill Development Mission in addressing India's demographic dividend and unemployment challenges.
Discuss the critical issues and gaps in India's skill ecosystem, such as skill-demand mismatch and quality of training, and suggest policy interventions.
Evaluate the role of government schemes like PMKVY and apprenticeship programs in enhancing the employability of Indian youth.
Examine how skill development can be aligned with the emerging demands of the digital economy and green jobs sector to ensure future-readiness of the workforce.
articles.imageDescription: A group of young people learning various vocational skills in a modern training center.
articles.trivia: India has the world's largest youth population, making skill development crucial for its economic growth.

articles.headline: Veteran Politician Manda Jagannath Passes Away at 73
articles.explainer:
• Manda Jagannath, a seasoned politician and former Member of Parliament from Andhra Pradesh, passed away at the age of 73.
• He served multiple terms in the Lok Sabha, representing the Nagarkurnool constituency.
• Known for his strong advocacy for farmers' rights and rural development, he played a key role in various parliamentary committees.
• His political career spanned several decades, contributing significantly to regional politics and national discourse.
• Leaders from across the political spectrum expressed condolences, remembering his dedication to public service.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Manda Jagannath was a former Member of Parliament.
He represented the Nagarkurnool constituency.
He was associated with politics in Andhra Pradesh (and later Telangana).
articles.imageDescription: A black and white portrait of Manda Jagannath.
articles.trivia: Lok Sabha members are directly elected by the people through universal adult franchise.

articles.headline: Former Senior Police Officer Abdul Sathar Kunju Passes Away at 85
articles.explainer:
• Abdul Sathar Kunju, a distinguished former police officer who served with distinction in various capacities, passed away at the age of 85.
• He was known for his integrity, dedication to law enforcement, and significant contributions to maintaining peace and order.
• His career included pivotal roles in state police forces and central agencies, where he handled several high-profile cases.
• Kunju was admired for his leadership and commitment to police reforms throughout his service.
• Colleagues and public figures expressed grief, remembering his exemplary service to the nation.
gsSubTopics: Role of civil services in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: Abdul Sathar Kunju was a former senior police officer.
He served in various capacities in law enforcement.
His career highlights included contributions to maintaining law and order.
articles.imageDescription: A black and white photograph of Abdul Sathar Kunju in uniform.
articles.trivia: The Indian Police Service (IPS) is one of the three All India Services, recruiting officers for various police forces nationwide.

articles.headline: Former Andhra Pradesh Minister Palavalasa Rajasekharam Passes Away at 80
articles.explainer:
• Palavalasa Rajasekharam, a veteran leader and former minister in Andhra Pradesh, passed away at the age of 80.
• He had a long and distinguished career in state politics, serving in various ministerial portfolios.
• Rajasekharam was instrumental in several developmental projects in his home state and was known for his grassroots connect.
• His demise marks the end of an era in regional politics, with leaders mourning his contributions to public life.
• He was highly regarded for his commitment to welfare programs and social justice.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Palavalasa Rajasekharam was a former minister in Andhra Pradesh.
He was a veteran state-level politician.
He contributed to developmental projects in Andhra Pradesh.
articles.imageDescription: A formal portrait of Palavalasa Rajasekharam.
articles.trivia: State ministers are part of the Council of Ministers in a state, headed by the Chief Minister.

articles.headline: Government Initiatives Boost Revival of Traditional Indian Performing Arts
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture announced new schemes and grants aimed at preserving and promoting traditional Indian performing arts forms.
• The initiatives focus on supporting ailing art forms, providing financial assistance to artists, and establishing new training centers.
• Special emphasis is placed on digitizing archives of endangered art forms and creating platforms for young artists to learn and perform.
• Cultural exchange programs and national festivals are being organized to showcase India's rich artistic heritage globally.
• The move is part of a broader effort to strengthen India's soft power and ensure the continuity of its diverse cultural traditions.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: India has a rich heritage of traditional performing arts (e.g., Classical Dances like Bharatanatyam, Kathak; Folk Dances, Classical Music like Hindustani, Carnatic).
The Sangeet Natak Akademi is India's national academy for music, dance, and drama.
UNESCO recognizes several Indian intangible cultural heritage elements (e.g., Kutiyattam, Vedic Chanting, Yoga, Kumbh Mela, Durga Puja).
Government schemes often provide grants to artists and cultural organizations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of traditional Indian performing arts in preserving cultural identity and promoting national unity.
Analyze the challenges faced by traditional artists and art forms in the contemporary era, including declining patronage and modernization.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives and policies aimed at the revival and promotion of India's cultural heritage.
Examine how cultural diplomacy and soft power can be leveraged through the promotion of Indian performing arts on the global stage.
articles.imageDescription: A Bharatanatyam dancer performing on stage, showcasing the elegance of Indian classical arts.
articles.trivia: There are eight recognized classical dance forms in India: Bharatanatyam, Kathak, Kathakali, Kuchipudi, Odissi, Manipuri, Mohiniyattam, and Sattriya.

articles.headline: India to Host G20 Startup Summit 2025: Fostering Global Innovation Ecosystem
articles.explainer:
• India announced its plan to host the G20 Startup Summit in 2025, bringing together global innovators, investors, and policymakers.
• The summit aims to foster cross-border collaboration, facilitate funding for emerging startups, and address regulatory challenges in the innovation landscape.
• Discussions will focus on sustainable innovation, digital transformation, and inclusive growth through startup ecosystems.
• India, a leading startup hub, seeks to leverage its G20 presidency to promote a global framework for supporting entrepreneurship.
• The event is expected to attract significant international participation and generate new partnerships.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The G20 (Group of Twenty) is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
It addresses major issues related to the global economy.
India held the G20 presidency in 2023.
India is home to the third-largest startup ecosystem globally.
Startup India is a flagship initiative of the Government of India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India hosting the G20 Startup Summit 2025 for its economic diplomacy and global leadership aspirations.
Discuss how India's burgeoning startup ecosystem can contribute to global innovation and address sustainable development goals.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for cross-border collaboration and investment in startups, particularly in emerging technologies.
Suggest policy recommendations for strengthening the global startup ecosystem, ensuring inclusive growth and digital access for all.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing interconnected global networks converging on a startup icon, with G20 logo.
articles.trivia: The G20 represents about two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and over 75% of global trade.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy for Accelerating Solar Power Park Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy launched a revised policy for the development of Ultra Mega Solar Power Parks across India.
• The new policy aims to simplify land acquisition processes, provide attractive incentives for developers, and ensure grid connectivity.
• It introduces a 'plug-and-play' model to reduce risks for developers and accelerate project completion.
• The initiative is crucial for achieving India's ambitious renewable energy targets and reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Focus will also be on promoting domestic manufacturing of solar components under the 'Make in India' initiative.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: India aims to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
Solar energy is a key component of India's renewable energy strategy.
Solar Power Parks are large areas identified for setting up multiple solar power projects.
The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) is the nodal ministry.
The International Solar Alliance (ISA) was co-founded by India and France.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and opportunities in accelerating solar power park development in India to meet its ambitious renewable energy targets.
Analyze the key features of the new policy for Ultra Mega Solar Power Parks and its potential impact on attracting investments and reducing project risks.
Evaluate the role of domestic manufacturing and 'Make in India' initiatives in strengthening India's solar energy sector and achieving energy security.
Examine the environmental and economic benefits of transitioning to renewable energy sources, particularly solar, for India's sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: Rows of solar panels stretching into the horizon under a clear sky, symbolizing large-scale solar power generation.
articles.trivia: Bhadla Solar Park in Rajasthan is one of the world's largest fully operational solar PV projects.

highlights: National Youth Day 2025 celebrated across India, honoring Swami Vivekananda and focusing on youth empowerment for 'Viksit Bharat'.
Parliamentary Committee begins review of draft Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025, aiming for stricter compliance.
ISRO successfully conducts critical test for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology, advancing cost-effective space access.
India and Japan hold 15th Strategic Dialogue, reaffirming commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
Supreme Court mandates stricter compliance and transparency in Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process to curb ecological degradation.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Youth Day in India, consider the following statements:
1. It is celebrated annually on January 12th to commemorate the birth anniversary of Swami Vivekananda.
2. The day was officially declared by the Government of India in 1985.
3. Swami Vivekananda's original name was Mool Shankar.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. National Youth Day is observed on January 12th, Swami Vivekananda's birth anniversary. Statement 2 is correct. The Government of India declared 12th January as National Youth Day in 1985. Statement 3 is incorrect. Swami Vivekananda's original name was Narendranath Datta. Mool Shankar was the original name of Swami Dayanand Saraswati.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is primarily responsible for administering Project Tiger in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is a statutory body under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, constituted under the provisions of the Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972, for strengthening tiger conservation, as per the powers and functions assigned to it under the said Act. It administers Project Tiger.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023:
1. It primarily aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data within India.
2. The Act mandates the establishment of a Data Protection Board of India.
3. Cross-border data transfers are explicitly prohibited under this Act.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The DPDP Act 2023 aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data in India. Statement 2 is correct. The Act mandates the establishment of the Data Protection Board of India. Statement 3 is incorrect. The Act does not explicitly prohibit cross-border data transfers; instead, it provides a framework for such transfers, subject to certain conditions and notifications.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a core principle of quantum computing?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Superposition, entanglement, and quantum tunnelling are all fundamental principles or phenomena in quantum mechanics that are leveraged in quantum computing. Deterministic computation, where the output is uniquely determined by the input, is characteristic of classical computers. Quantum computers, due to the probabilistic nature of quantum mechanics, often involve probabilistic outcomes, even if the underlying quantum evolution is deterministic.

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to the 'Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR)' often seen in news, which of the following statements best describes its concept?
options: (a) It is a policy approach under which producers are given significant responsibility for the treatment or disposal of post-consumer products.
(b) It refers to the responsibility of consumers to extend the life of products through proper maintenance.
(c) It is a scheme where the government extends financial support to producers for environmentally friendly manufacturing processes.
(d) It is a regulation that extends the warranty period for electronic goods by manufacturers.
prelimsMocks.answer: (a) It is a policy approach under which producers are given significant responsibility for the treatment or disposal of post-consumer products.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is a policy principle that assigns producers of products the responsibility for the entire life cycle of the products, including their take-back, recycling, and final disposal. This shifts the burden from municipalities and taxpayers to the producers and consumers, encouraging producers to design more environmentally friendly products.

mainsMocks.question: Swami Vivekananda's philosophy remains highly relevant for addressing contemporary challenges faced by Indian youth. Discuss how his ideals can inspire youth empowerment and nation-building in the context of 'Viksit Bharat @2047'. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Indian culture; GS2: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Human Resources.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Swami Vivekananda and National Youth Day. Explain his core ideals (self-reliance, character, social service, spiritual strength). Connect these ideals to modern challenges (unemployment, mental health, ethical values). Discuss how these can drive youth participation in 'Viksit Bharat' through innovation, entrepreneurship, and responsible citizenship. Conclude with the enduring legacy.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's emphasis on stricter compliance and transparency in the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process is a crucial step towards sustainable development. Analyze the existing shortcomings in India's EIA framework and suggest reforms to ensure its effectiveness in protecting ecological sensitive areas. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; GS2: Government policies and interventions.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by defining EIA and its importance. Highlight the Supreme Court's intervention and its rationale. Discuss current shortcomings: lack of independent review, inadequate public participation, post-clearance monitoring issues, cumulative impact neglect. Suggest reforms: independent expert committees, mandatory public hearings, robust digital monitoring, capacity building, legal strengthening, and integration with regional planning.

mainsMocks.question: India's 'Atmanirbhar Agritech Mission' aims to leverage technology for agricultural modernization. Critically evaluate the potential of integrating AI, IoT, and precision farming in transforming Indian agriculture, considering both the opportunities and the challenges for small and marginal farmers. (10 marks, 150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Major crops cropping patterns; e-technology in the aid of farmers; Food processing and related industries; Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Atmanirbhar Agritech Mission. Discuss opportunities: increased productivity, resource optimization (water, fertilizer), climate resilience, reduced losses, better market access. Acknowledge challenges: high initial cost, digital literacy, fragmented landholdings, lack of infrastructure, data privacy concerns. Suggest solutions: subsidies, FPO role, localized tech, government support for digital infrastructure, skilling.

quickBytes.text: National Youth Day (Jan 12) is celebrated on Swami Vivekananda's birth anniversary.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) was enacted in 2023.
ISRO's RLV (Reusable Launch Vehicle) program aims for cost-effective space access.
India and Japan are key partners in the Quad grouping.
EIA (Environmental Impact Assessment) is backed by the Environment Protection Act, 1986.
Project Tiger was launched in 1973 for tiger conservation.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets India's policy rates, headed by the RBI Governor.
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) makes producers responsible for product end-of-life.
The National Quantum Computing Mission aims to develop indigenous quantum tech.
India is home to the world's third-largest startup ecosystem.

GS1.headline: National Youth Day 2025: India Celebrates Swami Vivekananda's Legacy with Focus on Youth Empowerment
GS1.explainer:
• India observes National Youth Day on January 12th, marking the birth anniversary of Swami Vivekananda, a towering figure who inspired generations.
• The theme for 2025 focuses on 'Youth for Viksit Bharat', emphasizing their role in achieving a developed India by 2047.
• Nationwide events, seminars, and workshops are being organized to promote Vivekananda's ideals of self-reliance, character building, and social service among the youth.
• Prime Minister addressed a virtual conclave, urging youth to embrace innovation, entrepreneurship, and contribute actively to nation-building.
• Government initiatives like Skill India Mission and Start-up India were highlighted as platforms for youth to realize their potential.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.

History of the world will include events from 18th century such as industrial revolution, world wars, redrawal of national boundaries, colonization, decolonization, political philosophies like communism, capitalism, socialism etc.— their forms and effect on the society.
prelimsPoints: National Youth Day is celebrated on January 12th.
It commemorates the birth anniversary of Swami Vivekananda (born 1863).
The day was first celebrated in 1985.
Swami Vivekananda's original name was Narendranath Datta.
He was a chief disciple of Ramakrishna Paramahamsa.
He established the Ramakrishna Mission in 1897.
He represented India at the Parliament of the World's Religions in Chicago in 1893.
mainsPoints: Discuss Swami Vivekananda's philosophy and its relevance in contemporary India, particularly for youth development and nation-building.
Analyze the role of youth in achieving the 'Viksit Bharat @2047' vision, citing government initiatives and challenges.
Examine how the ideals of self-reliance, character building, and social service promoted by Vivekananda can address modern societal issues among youth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government programs aimed at youth empowerment, skill development, and entrepreneurship in light of Vivekananda's vision.
GS1.imageDescription: Illustration of Swami Vivekananda with a group of diverse Indian youth, symbolizing inspiration and future leadership.
GS1.trivia: Swami Vivekananda's famous speech at the Parliament of the World's Religions began with 'Sisters and Brothers of America...'.

GS1.headline: Government Initiatives Boost Revival of Traditional Indian Performing Arts
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture announced new schemes and grants aimed at preserving and promoting traditional Indian performing arts forms.
• The initiatives focus on supporting ailing art forms, providing financial assistance to artists, and establishing new training centers.
• Special emphasis is placed on digitizing archives of endangered art forms and creating platforms for young artists to learn and perform.
• Cultural exchange programs and national festivals are being organized to showcase India's rich artistic heritage globally.
• The move is part of a broader effort to strengthen India's soft power and ensure the continuity of its diverse cultural traditions.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: India has a rich heritage of traditional performing arts (e.g., Classical Dances like Bharatanatyam, Kathak; Folk Dances, Classical Music like Hindustani, Carnatic).
The Sangeet Natak Akademi is India's national academy for music, dance, and drama.
UNESCO recognizes several Indian intangible cultural heritage elements (e.g., Kutiyattam, Vedic Chanting, Yoga, Kumbh Mela, Durga Puja).
Government schemes often provide grants to artists and cultural organizations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of traditional Indian performing arts in preserving cultural identity and promoting national unity.
Analyze the challenges faced by traditional artists and art forms in the contemporary era, including declining patronage and modernization.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives and policies aimed at the revival and promotion of India's cultural heritage.
Examine how cultural diplomacy and soft power can be leveraged through the promotion of Indian performing arts on the global stage.
GS1.imageDescription: A Bharatanatyam dancer performing on stage, showcasing the elegance of Indian classical arts.
GS1.trivia: There are eight recognized classical dance forms in India: Bharatanatyam, Kathak, Kathakali, Kuchipudi, Odissi, Manipuri, Mohiniyattam, and Sattriya.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Committee to Review Draft Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025
GS2.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) is set to commence a detailed review of the draft rules for the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The rules, expected to be finalized by mid-2025, will lay down the operational framework for data fiduciaries and data principals.
• Key areas of discussion include consent mechanisms, data breach notification protocols, cross-border data transfers, and the powers of the Data Protection Board.
• Stakeholder consultations are ongoing to ensure a balanced approach that protects individual privacy while fostering innovation in the digital economy.
• The review aims to address concerns regarding implementation challenges and potential impacts on businesses and citizens.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) was enacted in 2023.

It aims to provide for the processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process personal data for lawful purposes.
The Act establishes a Data Protection Board of India.
Key principles include consent-based data processing, purpose limitation, and data minimization.
It applies to the processing of digital personal data within India and to certain processing outside India.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the potential impact of the DPDP Act and its upcoming rules on individual privacy rights and the digital economy in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing a robust data protection framework in a digitally diverse country like India.
Evaluate the role of the Data Protection Board of India in enforcing compliance and adjudicating disputes under the DPDP Act.
Compare India's data protection framework with international standards like GDPR, highlighting similarities and differences.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized image showing digital data flowing through a secure network, with a 'privacy shield' icon.
GS2.trivia: The concept of 'right to be forgotten' is included in data protection laws globally, allowing individuals to request deletion of their personal data under certain conditions.

GS2.headline: India and Japan Hold 15th Strategic Dialogue, Reaffirm Commitment to Free and Open Indo-Pacific
GS2.explainer:
• India and Japan conducted their 15th Strategic Dialogue in Tokyo, focusing on enhancing bilateral cooperation and regional security.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing adherence to international law and peaceful resolution of disputes.
• Discussions covered defense cooperation, economic partnership, infrastructure development, and collaboration in emerging technologies.
• The dialogue also addressed regional challenges, including maritime security, counter-terrorism, and climate change.
• Ministers underscored the importance of Quad cooperation and strengthening supply chain resilience.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are key partners in the Quad grouping, along with the USA and Australia.
The Indo-Pacific region is a major focus for both countries' foreign policy.
Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.
Both countries participate in joint military exercises, such as 'Dharma Guardian' (Army) and 'Malabar' (Naval - Quad).
The 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' defines India-Japan ties.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership in ensuring stability and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the various pillars of India-Japan cooperation, including defense, economy, and technology, and their contributions to India's strategic interests.
Evaluate the role of the Quad in addressing contemporary geopolitical challenges in the Indo-Pacific and its implications for India's foreign policy.
Examine how India and Japan can further enhance their collaboration in areas like critical technologies, climate action, and resilient supply chains.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed prominently during a high-level diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: Japan is India's largest ODA (Official Development Assistance) provider.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for Climate Resilience at UN Forum
GS2.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to global climate action and pledged enhanced support for climate resilience initiatives at a key UN forum.
• The Indian delegation highlighted its domestic efforts in renewable energy deployment, afforestation, and disaster risk reduction.
• Emphasis was placed on the need for developed countries to fulfill their financial commitments to developing nations for climate adaptation and mitigation.
• India also advocated for technology transfer and capacity building to help vulnerable countries combat climate change impacts.
• The discussions focused on preparing for the upcoming COP30 summit and accelerating the implementation of the Paris Agreement goals.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India is a signatory to the Paris Agreement.
India's Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) include reducing emissions intensity and increasing non-fossil fuel electricity capacity.
The concept of 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities' (CBDR) is central to India's stance in climate negotiations.
The Green Climate Fund (GCF) is a mechanism under the UNFCCC to assist developing countries in adaptation and mitigation practices.
India has set ambitious targets for renewable energy capacity, particularly solar power.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's leadership role in global climate action, specifically its contributions to climate resilience and adaptation efforts.
Discuss the challenges faced by developing countries, including India, in achieving climate targets without adequate financial and technological support from developed nations.
Evaluate the effectiveness of international climate forums and agreements, such as the Paris Agreement, in fostering equitable and ambitious climate action.
Examine how India's domestic policies and initiatives, like the National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC), contribute to its international climate pledges.
GS2.imageDescription: A globe surrounded by hands reaching out, symbolizing international cooperation on climate action, with renewable energy sources in the background.
GS2.trivia: India's per capita emissions are significantly lower than those of many developed countries.

GS2.headline: Review of National Skill Development Mission: Progress and Future Targets for Youth
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship conducted a comprehensive review of the National Skill Development Mission (NSDM) progress.
• The review highlighted achievements in skilling millions of youth across various sectors and the expansion of ITIs and Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Kendras.
• Challenges such as skill-demand mismatch, quality of training, and industry linkages were identified as areas requiring further focus.
• New targets were set to align skilling programs with emerging industry demands, particularly in green jobs and digital technologies.
• Emphasis was placed on strengthening apprenticeship programs and promoting entrepreneurship among skilled youth.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
GS2.source: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
prelimsPoints: The National Skill Development Mission (NSDM) was launched in 2015.
It aims to create convergence across various skill development schemes.
Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) is a flagship scheme under NSDM.
The National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC) is a not-for-profit public limited company under the Ministry.
ITIs provide vocational training for various trades.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and achievements of the National Skill Development Mission in addressing India's demographic dividend and unemployment challenges.
Discuss the critical issues and gaps in India's skill ecosystem, such as skill-demand mismatch and quality of training, and suggest policy interventions.
Evaluate the role of government schemes like PMKVY and apprenticeship programs in enhancing the employability of Indian youth.
Examine how skill development can be aligned with the emerging demands of the digital economy and green jobs sector to ensure future-readiness of the workforce.
GS2.imageDescription: A group of young people learning various vocational skills in a modern training center.
GS2.trivia: India has the world's largest youth population, making skill development crucial for its economic growth.

GS2.headline: Veteran Politician Manda Jagannath Passes Away at 73
GS2.explainer:
• Manda Jagannath, a seasoned politician and former Member of Parliament from Andhra Pradesh, passed away at the age of 73.
• He served multiple terms in the Lok Sabha, representing the Nagarkurnool constituency.
• Known for his strong advocacy for farmers' rights and rural development, he played a key role in various parliamentary committees.
• His political career spanned several decades, contributing significantly to regional politics and national discourse.
• Leaders from across the political spectrum expressed condolences, remembering his dedication to public service.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Manda Jagannath was a former Member of Parliament.
He represented the Nagarkurnool constituency.
He was associated with politics in Andhra Pradesh (and later Telangana).
GS2.imageDescription: A black and white portrait of Manda Jagannath.
GS2.trivia: Lok Sabha members are directly elected by the people through universal adult franchise.

GS2.headline: Former Senior Police Officer Abdul Sathar Kunju Passes Away at 85
GS2.explainer:
• Abdul Sathar Kunju, a distinguished former police officer who served with distinction in various capacities, passed away at the age of 85.
• He was known for his integrity, dedication to law enforcement, and significant contributions to maintaining peace and order.
• His career included pivotal roles in state police forces and central agencies, where he handled several high-profile cases.
• Kunju was admired for his leadership and commitment to police reforms throughout his service.
• Colleagues and public figures expressed grief, remembering his exemplary service to the nation.
gsSubTopics: Role of civil services in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: Abdul Sathar Kunju was a former senior police officer.
He served in various capacities in law enforcement.
His career highlights included contributions to maintaining law and order.
GS2.imageDescription: A black and white photograph of Abdul Sathar Kunju in uniform.
GS2.trivia: The Indian Police Service (IPS) is one of the three All India Services, recruiting officers for various police forces nationwide.

GS2.headline: Former Andhra Pradesh Minister Palavalasa Rajasekharam Passes Away at 80
GS2.explainer:
• Palavalasa Rajasekharam, a veteran leader and former minister in Andhra Pradesh, passed away at the age of 80.
• He had a long and distinguished career in state politics, serving in various ministerial portfolios.
• Rajasekharam was instrumental in several developmental projects in his home state and was known for his grassroots connect.
• His demise marks the end of an era in regional politics, with leaders mourning his contributions to public life.
• He was highly regarded for his commitment to welfare programs and social justice.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Palavalasa Rajasekharam was a former minister in Andhra Pradesh.
He was a veteran state-level politician.
He contributed to developmental projects in Andhra Pradesh.
GS2.imageDescription: A formal portrait of Palavalasa Rajasekharam.
GS2.trivia: State ministers are part of the Council of Ministers in a state, headed by the Chief Minister.

GS2.headline: India to Host G20 Startup Summit 2025: Fostering Global Innovation Ecosystem
GS2.explainer:
• India announced its plan to host the G20 Startup Summit in 2025, bringing together global innovators, investors, and policymakers.
• The summit aims to foster cross-border collaboration, facilitate funding for emerging startups, and address regulatory challenges in the innovation landscape.
• Discussions will focus on sustainable innovation, digital transformation, and inclusive growth through startup ecosystems.
• India, a leading startup hub, seeks to leverage its G20 presidency to promote a global framework for supporting entrepreneurship.
• The event is expected to attract significant international participation and generate new partnerships.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The G20 (Group of Twenty) is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
It addresses major issues related to the global economy.
India held the G20 presidency in 2023.
India is home to the third-largest startup ecosystem globally.
Startup India is a flagship initiative of the Government of India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India hosting the G20 Startup Summit 2025 for its economic diplomacy and global leadership aspirations.
Discuss how India's burgeoning startup ecosystem can contribute to global innovation and address sustainable development goals.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for cross-border collaboration and investment in startups, particularly in emerging technologies.
Suggest policy recommendations for strengthening the global startup ecosystem, ensuring inclusive growth and digital access for all.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing interconnected global networks converging on a startup icon, with G20 logo.
GS2.trivia: The G20 represents about two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and over 75% of global trade.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful completion of a crucial test for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.
• The test involved a scaled-down model performing autonomous landing maneuvers, simulating re-entry conditions.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing a cost-effective and sustainable space transportation system for India.
• RLV technology aims to reduce launch costs by recovering and reusing the first and second stages of rockets, similar to SpaceX's Falcon 9.
• The long-term vision includes developing a Two Stage To Orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch vehicle.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO is developing a Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program.
RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) is a key project under this program.
The goal is to achieve cost-effective access to space.
RLV technology involves recovering and reusing launch vehicle stages.
The RLV-TD program includes several experimental missions: Hypersonic Flight Experiment (HEX), Landing Experiment (LEX), Return Flight Experiment (REX), and Scramjet Propulsion Experiment (SPEX).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's future space missions and its implications for global space competitiveness.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of developing reusable rockets compared to conventional single-use launch vehicles.
Examine the challenges and technological hurdles ISRO faces in developing and operationalizing a fully reusable launch system.
How will RLV technology contribute to India's ambition of becoming a major player in commercial space launches and space tourism?
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype descending for an autonomous landing.
GS3.trivia: The Space Shuttle was the world's first operational reusable orbital spacecraft, though its reusability was complex and costly.

GS3.headline: Supreme Court Directs Stricter Compliance and Transparency in Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA)
GS3.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, emphasized the need for stricter compliance and enhanced transparency in the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process.
• The judgment directed regulatory bodies to ensure thorough public hearings and independent scrutiny of EIA reports before granting project clearances.
• It highlighted the importance of cumulative impact assessments for projects in ecologically sensitive zones.
• The court also mandated the establishment of a robust post-clearance monitoring mechanism to ensure adherence to environmental conditions.
• This ruling aims to strengthen environmental governance and prevent ecological degradation caused by industrial and infrastructure projects.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: EIA is a process of evaluating the likely environmental impacts of a proposed project or development, taking into account inter-related socio-economic, cultural and human-health impacts, both beneficial and adverse.
In India, EIA is statutorily backed by the Environment Protection Act, 1986.
The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) is the nodal agency.
Public consultation/hearing is a mandatory part of the EIA process for certain projects.
The Supreme Court often intervenes to ensure environmental protection through its rulings.
mainsPoints: Analyze the critical role of Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) in sustainable development and environmental protection in India.
Discuss the loopholes and challenges in the current EIA process that necessitate judicial intervention, as highlighted by the Supreme Court's ruling.
Evaluate the implications of stricter EIA compliance and increased transparency for industrial development and investment in India.
Suggest reforms to strengthen the EIA framework in India, incorporating best practices from around the world.
GS3.imageDescription: A gavel striking a sound block placed on a document titled 'EIA Report', symbolizing judicial scrutiny over environmental clearances.
GS3.trivia: The first formal EIA system was established in the United States with the National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) of 1969.

GS3.headline: Government Launches 'Atmanirbhar Agritech Mission' to Modernize Indian Agriculture
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare launched the 'Atmanirbhar Agritech Mission' aimed at revolutionizing Indian agriculture through technology.
• The mission focuses on promoting precision farming, use of AI and IoT in agriculture, climate-resilient crop varieties, and digital extension services.
• It includes provisions for subsidies and incentives for farmers to adopt modern agricultural machinery and practices.
• Special emphasis will be placed on developing indigenous agricultural technologies and strengthening farmer-producer organizations (FPOs).
• The initiative seeks to enhance farm productivity, reduce post-harvest losses, and ensure better income for farmers.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: The mission aligns with the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision.
Focuses on integrating technology like AI, IoT, drones in agriculture.
Aims to enhance productivity and reduce post-harvest losses.
Promotes climate-resilient agricultural practices.
Includes support for Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs).
mainsPoints: Analyze how the 'Atmanirbhar Agritech Mission' can address the challenges faced by Indian agriculture, such as low productivity, climate change impacts, and farmer distress.
Discuss the role of technology (AI, IoT, precision farming) in transforming Indian agriculture and contributing to food security and farmer income.
Evaluate the potential of indigenous agricultural technologies and FPOs in achieving the mission's objectives.
Examine the policy measures and investments required to ensure widespread adoption of modern agricultural practices under this mission.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer using a tablet to monitor crop health, with drones flying over fields in the background, symbolizing modern agriculture.
GS3.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of rice, wheat, sugarcane, and cotton.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Policy Rates Amidst Global Economic Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its first meeting of 2025, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach amidst persistent global economic uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions and fluctuating commodity prices.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need to balance inflation management with supporting economic growth, which is showing signs of resilience.
• The MPC also kept the stance of 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Future policy actions will remain data-dependent, with a close watch on domestic inflation trends and global financial market stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body headed by the RBI Governor.
Its primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The current inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
The 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance implies that the RBI will focus on reducing liquidity to control inflation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on policy rates, considering both domestic economic indicators and global headwinds.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the objective of supporting economic growth in the current macroeconomic environment.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflation and influencing economic activity in India.
Examine the implications of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance for India's financial markets and broader economy in the short to medium term.
GS3.imageDescription: A close-up of the Reserve Bank of India logo on a building facade, symbolizing monetary authority.
GS3.trivia: The RBI was established in 1935 under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and was nationalized in 1949.

GS3.headline: New National Policy on E-Waste Management Announced to Tackle Growing Electronic Waste
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change unveiled a comprehensive National Policy on E-Waste Management, effective 2025.
• The policy aims to streamline the collection, segregation, recycling, and disposal of electronic waste across the country.
• It introduces stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) norms, holding manufacturers accountable for the entire lifecycle of their products.
• Provisions for incentivizing formal recycling units and discouraging informal, environmentally harmful practices are also included.
• The policy emphasizes public awareness campaigns and skill development for safe e-waste handling and recycling.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: E-waste is defined as discarded electrical or electronic devices.
India's first E-Waste (Management and Handling) Rules were notified in 2011.
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) makes producers responsible for managing their products at end-of-life.
Improper e-waste disposal can lead to soil and water contamination due to heavy metals and toxic chemicals.
The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) oversees e-waste management in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing challenge of e-waste management in India and the environmental and health hazards associated with improper disposal.
Analyze the key provisions of the new National Policy on E-Waste Management and evaluate its potential to transform India's approach to electronic waste.
Examine the role of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) in promoting a circular economy and sustainable consumption patterns.
Suggest measures to strengthen the informal e-waste recycling sector and integrate it into a formal, environmentally sound management system.
GS3.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic devices, with a recycling symbol superimposed, illustrating the e-waste problem.
GS3.trivia: Globally, e-waste is the fastest-growing waste stream.

GS3.headline: Project Tiger Completes 50 Years: Achievements and Future Conservation Strategies
GS3.explainer:
• Project Tiger, India's flagship wildlife conservation program, marks its 50th anniversary, celebrating significant achievements in tiger population recovery.
• A comprehensive review of the project highlighted the increase in tiger numbers and the expansion of protected areas.
